top_signals.title: Bahoshy: Mideast Venture Funding Plunges in 1H
top_signals.summary: Private capital data platform, Magnitt, says Middle East venture funding fell 22% in the first half of the year, with actual deal count plunging by 41%. Many of the deals were signed before the US-Israel attacks on Iran. CEO Philip Bahoshy told Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa that the real test will hit this summer. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnout, frustration and heartbreak: Amazon layoffs take their toll in saturated job market
top_signals.summary: In the eight-plus months since Amazon announced its most expansive job cuts ever, laid off workers have been thrust into an increasingly saturated labor market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Healthcare Looks Ready to Star in Rotation Trade
top_signals.summary: A compelling argument is growing for healthcare stocks to be among the big winners in the rotation to more defensive parts of the market as the sector’s outlook improves.
